paper_title: MoDA: Modulation Adapter for Fine-Grained Visual Grounding in Instructional MLLMs

method.name: Modulation Adapter (MoDA)
method.description: A lightweight, plug-and-play module that modulates visual embeddings via cross-attention using language tokens as guidance
key_features: Does not modify the underlying architecture
Improves alignment between vision and language representations
method.context: Part of Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) that integrate pretrained visual encoders with large language models via lightweight adapter modules

problem_addressed.main_issue: State-of-the-art MLLMs struggle with fine-grained visual grounding, leading to hallucinations in scenes requiring detailed reasoning over visual inputs
problem_addressed.definition_of_hallucinations: Model outputs that are inconsistent with the actual image semantics
problem_addressed.identified_bottleneck: CLIP-based visual encoder, commonly employed in MLLM, due to its patch-based representations that fail to capture localized details

results_example.illustration: A 3×3 grid simulating CLIP's visual tokenization over a sleeping French bulldog clutching a plush toy
findings: None of the patches contain uniform and unique visual elements
Patch 5 contains the dog's torso together with part of the stuffed toy and the cushioned bed
Patch 6 covers the dog's head and ear resting on the hardwood floor
Multiple semantic elements are embedded into a single high-dimensional representation
When tokens blend unrelated concepts, it becomes harder for attention mechanisms to disentangle them

title: MoDA: Modulation Adapter for Fine-Grained Visual Grounding in Instructional MLLMs
